title: Apple Inc. (AAPL) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Apple Inc (AAPL) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.
thesisTitle: The AI Thesis: The Ecosystem Toll Road
thesisParagraphs: Apple's transition from a pure hardware company to a services-oriented ecosystem is largely complete, yet the market still heavily penalizes it for fluctuations in iPhone units sold. This is a mistake.

The true value of Apple lies in its installed base of over 2.2 billion active devices. This base functions as an inescapable digital toll road, funneling incredibly high-margin recurring revenue through iCloud, the App Store, Apple Music, and Apple Pay. While hardware growth may be slowing, the monetization of the existing user base continues to compound. My valuation model heavily weights this high-margin Service revenue stream, projecting it will continue to buoy overall Free Cash Flow even amidst cyclical hardware downturns, bolstered in the short-to-medium term by the AI-driven "Apple Intelligence" upgrade cycle.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: An 8.5% growth rate reflects a blended projection. While iPhone upgrade cycles have elongated, Services revenue continues to grow at double digits with gross margins exceeding 70%. Furthermore, the rollout of Apple Intelligence features tied strictly to newer silicon will force a modest, multi-year hardware upgrade supercycle, lifting overall FCF.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: 8.2% is an appropriately low discount rate for a company that generates over $100B in FCF annually. Given the 10-year treasury at 4.18%, this implies a very low equity risk premium, justified by Apple's unparalleled brand loyalty. However, the rate is not lower due to the tangible risk of regulatory actions forcing changes to App Store fee structures.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 3.0% is a conservative terminal rate. It sits slightly below historical global GDP growth, acknowledging the law of large numbers—it is mathematically difficult for a $3.5T+ enterprise to grow much faster than the economy itself into perpetuity, despite its formidable moat.

risks.introText: While my valuation leans moderately bullish, several structural risks could materially impact Apple's intrinsic value trajectory:

items.title: 1. Geopolitical Supply Chain Risk
items.description: Despite diversification efforts into India and Vietnam, Apple's manufacturing base and a significant portion of its end-market revenue remain deeply tied to China. Escalating trade tensions or tariffs could severely compress gross margins.

items.title: 2. App Store Antitrust Regulation
items.description: Regulators in the EU and the US are actively challenging the 30% App Store "tax" and the restriction of third-party payment systems. A forced dismantling of this walled garden would directly impact Apple's highest-margin Services revenue.

items.title: 3. The Search Engine Deal
items.description: The DOJ's antitrust case against Google threatens the $20B+ annual payment Apple receives for making Google the default search engine on iOS. Losing this pure-profit revenue stream would instantly impair Free Cash Flow.

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick an 8.5% growth rate for Apple?
faq.answer: Gemini projects that while hardware sales might see slower upgrade cycles, the high-margin Services segment continues to grow reliably at double digits. Additionally, Apple Intelligence is expected to drive a modest hardware upgrade supercycle over the next 2-3 years, justifying a blended 8.5% free cash flow growth rate.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for Apple's DCF?
faq.answer: An 8.2% discount rate was selected. This reflects a 4.18% risk-free rate and Apple's incredibly reliable cash generation, balanced against the increasing regulatory scrutiny on the App Store and broader antitrust concerns.

faq.question: Is it safe to rely on AI for stock valuation?
faq.answer: No. This analysis is a demonstration of AI reasoning based on a specific set of inputs and rigid formulas. It is not financial advice. AI models cannot predict regulatory actions, geopolitical shifts, or black swan economic events.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 17, 2026).
